confirmed.note: LuaWeatherBindings is a copy-renamed clone of LuaSeasonBindings: identical registerBindings scaffold (includes, sol guard, two null-check blocks with LOG_ERROR_C, create_named_table, lambda-wrapper-per-API idiom, section-comment banners); only Season<->Weather and the per-API verb names differ. Both files added in the SAME commit, so it is a single copy-paste-then-edit act.

confirmed.where: src/core/Engine.cpp Engine::createDemoEntities() (new function appended after setupNativeToolbar, ~lines 2010-2105 of new file)
confirmed.note: Three sequential entity-construction blocks (Red Cube / Green Sphere / Yellow Light), each a copy of the same scaffold: createEntity -> new TransformComponent(pos,rot,scale) -> addComponent -> new RendererComponent -> new MaterialComponent (set color/flags) -> sceneHierarchy->addNode(name) -> node->userData -> LOG_INFO_C. Only literal values/names change. Should be a loop over a small data table; classic AI within-file repetition.

false_positives.where: examples/camera_system.cpp / collision_detection.cpp / player_controller.cpp (and 7 sibling example files, head ~lines 1-30)
false_positives.what_happened: All 10 tutorial example files share a fixed scaffold: a doxygen /** @file ... @brief ... This example shows: */ header, the same include preamble (<iostream>,<memory>,glm), int main(), and a std::cout banner pattern (=== Fresh Voxel Engine: X === then [N/5] step lines). A token/line detector keyed on the banner + header template flags them as near-duplicates, but the actual bodies exercise entirely different engine APIs and logic. This is shared demo scaffolding, not lifted code.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add an examples/ + tutorial path filter (like the existing tests/ exclusion) OR strip leading comment/doxygen blocks and isolated I/O-banner statement runs (cout/printf-only lines) before fingerprinting, and require >=K non-comment, non-IO statements to overlap before reporting a chunk match.

false_positives.what_happened: GoogleTest fixture file: 20 TEST_F(SelectionRendererTest, ...) bodies repeat the same arrange/act/assert scaffold (construct SelectionRenderer, initialize with mock DebugRenderer, call render, EXPECT no-crash / EXPECT color). The per-test boilerplate is structurally near-identical by the nature of xUnit fixtures, so a chunk detector reports many internal near-dupes. Not copy-paste drift.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: exclude test sources (tests/**, *Tests.cpp, *_test.cpp) from chunk dedup by default, and when not excluded, treat TEST/TEST_F/SECTION macro-delimited bodies as a recognized fixture idiom (raise the similarity threshold / require a longer non-macro statement run inside the body to count).
